title: Savory Silk Coconut Soup
description: A silky, salty, egg-free Brazilian dish that's perfect for hangovers and prepared using sous-vide technology to achieve a smooth texture.

ingredients.name: 1 can full-fat coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's full-fat for a richer flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Use this to enhance the silky texture and saltiness.

ingredients.name: 4 slices of bacon, cooked and crumbled (optional)
ingredients.notes: For added flavor but optional for vegans

instructions: In a large pot, sauté the onions and garlic in the bacon fat until softened.
Add the stock, bay leaf, and salt. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 20 minutes.
Transfer the soup mixture into your sous-vide bag and seal tightly.
Place the bag in the water bath set at 160°F (71°C) for 2 hours.
While it cooks, combine coconut milk and heavy cream in a blender. Blend until smooth.
When done, let the soup cool to room temperature before blending with the coconut mixture.
Strain the blended soup through a fine-mesh sieve into serving bowls.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
